name: Tour of Bolón (PR-CV 196)
description: Hike the moderate 6.9-mile Return to Bolón (PR-CV 196) from Elba, a circular route with varied terrain and historical sights.
region_intro: Hiking around Elda, located in the Alicante region of Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The area features a blend of mountainous terrain, natural parks, and scenic routes. Characterized by mountains, including the Serra del Maigmó Natural Park, and paths through olive groves, the region provides varied environments for exploration. Trails often combine paved and unpaved sections, with some offering steeper ascents. Best hiking trails around Elda The most popular hiking route is Tour of Bolón (PR-CV 196), a 6.9 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route traverses diverse landscapes including Mediterranean forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de Salinas – Arco de Cámara loop from Alt de Camara, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This path offers views towards the Campo de Alicante. Local hikers also love the Forest Ranger's Hut – Cid-Basurero Trail loop from Elda, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and varied paths, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Elda is defined by mountainous terrain, natural parks, and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Elda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Elda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Elda?
A: Elda offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 60 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 8 easy, 52 moderate, and 6 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Elda?
A: Yes, Elda has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. For example, the region features paths through picturesque olive groves and less strenuous sections of the Vía Verde del Maigmó, which is a repurposed railway line offering gentle gradients and scenic views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Elda?
A: The terrain around Elda is quite diverse, ranging from mountainous areas within the Serra del Maigmó Natural Park to scenic routes through olive groves. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved paths, with some trails featuring steeper ascents, particularly in the more rugged mountain sections.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Elda?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Elda are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19384202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Elda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient way to explore the local landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Elda?
A: Elda's hiking trails offer access to various natural features. You can explore canyons like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5505101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, discover natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almortxó Sand Dunes&lt;/a&gt;, or reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3072247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Silla del Cid Summit&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also feature highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3363662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in the Barranco de la Encantada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Elda?
A: Many trails in the Elda region are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those in natural parks like Serra del Maigmó, where dogs are generally welcome on leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations for specific areas, but the diverse landscapes offer plenty of opportunities for you and your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Elda?
A: The best time to hike in Elda is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Elda?
A: Yes, Elda offers several family-friendly hiking options. The Vía Verde del Maigmó, for instance, provides a relatively flat and well-maintained path suitable for families with children. Many of the easier routes through olive groves also offer pleasant and accessible walks for all ages.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Elda?
A: The hiking experiences in Elda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to tranquil olive groves, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of challenge and scenic beauty.

Q: Can I find routes that offer great viewpoints in Elda?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain around Elda provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3957192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Salinas – Arco de Cámara loop from Alt de Camara&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas towards the Campo de Alicante, allowing hikers to appreciate the region's expansive beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that lead through the Serra del Maigmó Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Serra del Maigmó Natural Park is a significant hiking destination near Elda. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e863355536/tour-of-bolon-pr-cv-196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Bolón (PR-CV 196)&lt;/a&gt; traverse diverse landscapes, including Mediterranean forests characteristic of the park, offering a true immersion in its natural beauty.
tips: Routes to the Cross of Bolón de Elda in the Vinalopó region. Beautiful excursions with views of the Sierra del Caballo and El Cid.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Return to Bolón (PR-CV 196) from Elba trail?
faqs.answer: This hike is classified as moderate. While it's a circular route, a moderate rating typically implies some ascents and descents that require a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is the Return to Bolón (PR-CV 196) from Elba a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular trail, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins in the Jardines del Vinalopó. While specific parking details aren't provided, starting points in urban areas often have nearby street parking or designated lots. It's advisable to check local parking regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start from the Jardines del Vinalopó, pass through an underpass beneath train tracks, and follow a path above houses and caves in Polígono 25. The route continues past the Depósito de La Gervasia, descends towards the northwest side of Bolón through old quarries, and includes a section traversing the main quarries before returning via the "senda del Tío Elías."

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse scenery, including views from above the houses and caves of Polígono 25. Notable points include the Pinada del Trinitario (a pine forest), the intriguing Casa del Barranco del Capuchino (also known as "del Crimen"), and an abandoned dwelling called "casa de Noé." You'll also traverse significant quarry landscapes and pass the Cruz de Bolón.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares a path with the GR 7 initially. It also intersects with other official trails such as Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante, and Camino del Cid (Senderista) for sections of the journey.

faqs.question: Is the Return to Bolón (PR-CV 196) from Elba trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this trail are not detailed, many trails in the region are dog-friendly, often requiring dogs to be kept on a leash. It's always best to ensure your dog is well-behaved and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in this region, offering milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Return to Bolón (PR-CV 196) from Elba trail.

faqs.question: Are there any variants or alternative routes for this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR-CV 196 has several variants, including PR-CV 196-1, PR-CV 196-2, and PR-CV 196-3. These offer options for different lengths and destinations, such as reaching the summit of Bolón or exploring more of the Pinada del Trinitario.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this moderate hike?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike with varied terrain, it's recommended to wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, layered clothing suitable for the weather, and bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can also be helpful on some sections.
